title: Viking Therapeutics Completes VANQUISH-1 Trial Enrollment
text: Viking Therapeutics announced completion of patient enrollment in its Phase 3 VANQUISH-1 trial testing VK2735, a dual GLP-1/GIP receptor agonist for obesity treatment. The randomized, double-blind study enrolled approximately 4,650 adults with obesity or overweight status across four weekly subcutaneous injection arms (7.5mg, 12.5mg, 17.5mg doses, plus placebo). The 78-week treatment period will assess body weight reduction as the primary endpoint. The trial accrued rapidly and exceeded enrollment target, completing ahead of schedule and indicating strong market enthusiasm for obesity therapies beyond currently available options, according to CEO Brian Lian. Viking is simultaneously conducting the VANQUISH-2 trial in type 2 diabetes patients with obesity, expecting to complete enrollment in Q1 2026. The 2024 Phase 2 VENTURE study showed VK2735 achieved statistically significant body weight reductions up to 14.7% after 13 weekly doses, with predominantly mild-to-moderate adverse events and balanced discontinuation rates compared to placebo. VK2735 is in development as both oral and subcutaneous formulations for metabolic disorder treatment.
